commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ee611c4ad8122ee54661b653c1bd3fd60d9eb9f4)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds shared Convex download metering for skill and package downloads with HMAC identity hashing, daily dedupe/rollup tables, weekly snapshots, cron jobs, tests, and an internal spec note.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection is enough: the new HMAC helper throws when the secret is absent, and the changed download routes catch that failure after removing the legacy counter write path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This needs maintainer/member follow-up because the remaining blocker is an upgrade policy choice: preserve legacy counters on missing secret or require an explicit deploy/setup gate.

target.title: Add shared download metering
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a shared download-metering path for both skill downloads and package/plugin downloads.

Before this change, skill ZIP downloads and package/plugin downloads were counted differently:

- skill downloads were deduped by skill + identity + hour
- package/plugin downloads wrote raw package stat events, so repeated downloads could each increment the package download count

After this change, both paths use the same metering logic:

- identify the download target as either `skill` or `package`
- identify the requester as a user when available, otherwise by client IP
- HMAC-hash the identity before storing it
- count at most one download per target + identity + UTC day
- store one total `downloads` count, without splitting reporting into user-vs-IP buckets

## Behavior

The new metering path is best-effort. Download responses still succeed if metrics cannot be recorded.

The visible count model is intentionally simple:

```text
downloads
```

The user/IP distinction is only kept as metadata for dedupe and diagnostics:

```text
identityKind: "user" | "ip"
identityHash: string
```

This does not rewrite or estimate historical download counts. Existing public counters continue from newly recorded events after this PR is deployed.

## Data Model

Adds these Convex tables:

- `downloadMetricDedupes`
- `downloadMetricDailyRollups`
- `downloadMetricWeeklySnapshots`
- `downloadMetricGlobalWeeklySnapshots`

Daily rollups are append-only after dedupe. This avoids hot writes to a single target/day document during bursts.

Weekly snapshots are generated from the daily rollup rows and can be rerun for the same week because snapshot writes upsert by target/week and global week.

## Routes Updated

- skill ZIP downloads now schedule `downloadMetrics.recordDownloadMetricInternal`
- package legacy ZIP downloads now record package download metrics through the shared path
- ClawPack/npm tarball downloads now record package download metrics through the shared path
- npm-style package installs still record installs, and the downloaded tarball also contributes to deduped download metrics

## Operational Notes

New metering requires this Convex env var:

```text
DOWNLOAD_METERING_HMAC_SECRET
```

If the secret is missing, metering fails inside the best-effort metric path. The user still receives the download response, but no new download metric is counted.

New crons:

- prune old download metric dedupe rows
- generate weekly download metric snapshots for the previous complete UTC week

## Tests

- `bun run test -- convex/downloadMetrics.test.ts convex/downloads.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `bun run format:check`
- `bun run lint`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit`

Not run:

- `bun run test`
- `bun run build`
- `bun run coverage`
- `bunx convex deploy`
